mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is a coincident indicator of economic activity; a sustained month-over-month increase signals expanding output and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect is persistent across all phases of the business cycle.
failure_condition: When INDPRO increases month-over-month, SPX 20d returns are negative in >50% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 8% signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending expectations, leading to positive short-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 8% but SPX 5d returns are negative in >60% of such events over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near zero (below 0.25%), the market perceives a deeply accommodative monetary stance that compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% but SPX 20d returns are negative in >50% of cases over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises over three consecutive months while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, real interest rates decline, stimulating risk appetite and boosting equity prices over the next 20 trading days. The interaction of rising inflation and low nominal rates amplifies the positive signal.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases for three consecutive months and FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL rises for three months and FEDFUNDS <0.5%, but SPX 20d returns are negative in >55% of such windows over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crash recovery regime where housing starts (HOUST) are below 1 million units, a month-over-month increase in HOUST signals a strengthening housing market and broader economic recovery, driving positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days. In other regimes (e.g., above-trend HOUST), the relationship may weaken or reverse.
evidence_refs: fact: housing starts are a leading indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST rises >5% MoM while HOUST <1M, but SPX 60d returns are negative in >50% of such instances over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic output; positive quarter-over-quarter growth signals economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, which supports higher equity prices over the subsequent 60 trading days. This fundamental relationship is expected to hold consistently.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 increases quarter-over-quarter but SPX 60d returns are negative in >50% of quarters over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceed 200,000 in a given month, it indicates strong labor demand and economic momentum, which typically boosts investor confidence and equity prices over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: payrolls are closely watched by markets
failure_condition: If PAYEMS exceeds 200k but SPX 20d returns are negative in >40% of such months over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) increase month-over-month, it signals robust consumer demand across goods and housing, reinforcing each other's positive signal and driving higher equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST increase MoM but SPX 20d returns are negative in >50% of such months over any 3-year rolling window.
